title: Kanban Board
subtitle: Kanban view of all agents by status
subtitleSessions: Kanban view of all sessions by status
noAgentsDesc: Start a Claude Code session with hooks installed to see agents appear here.
noSessionsDesc: Start a Claude Code session with hooks installed to see sessions appear here.

agent.working:
Agent is actively running a tool call right now.

Entered on PreToolUse or UserPromptSubmit, exits on Stop (turn finished).
agent.waiting:
Agent finished its turn and is blocked on the human - either Claude is waiting for the next user message or has prompted for permission to use a tool.

Cleared on the next PreToolUse / PostToolUse, or when the session ends.
agent.completed:
Agent has fully ended for this session - typically because SessionEnd fired or, for subagents, SubagentStop was processed.

No further activity will be recorded against this agent.
agent.error: Agent recorded an error and will not continue. For main agents this comes from a Stop with stop_reason=error; for subagents, from a failed run.

session.active: Session is mid-turn - Claude is currently running and the user isn't blocking anything. Brief by nature: a session moves through Active during tool calls and back to Waiting at end of turn.
session.waiting:
Session is sitting on the user. Either Claude finished its turn (most common) or it has surfaced a permission prompt and is blocked until the human responds.

Look here for sessions you owe a reply to.
session.completed:
Session has fully closed - SessionEnd fired (CLI exited cleanly) or the user explicitly closed it.

All agents on this session are also completed.
session.error: Session ended with an error - Claude returned stop_reason=error, an unrecoverable failure occurred, or an upstream API error terminated the run.
session.abandoned: Session went stale without a clean SessionEnd. Marked abandoned after 5+ minutes of no activity (orphaned by /resume, network drop, or the CLI being killed). Distinct from Completed: we never saw a real exit signal.
